name: Keith Jarrett
abstract: Keith Jarrett (born May 8, 1945) is an American pianist and composer. Jarrett started his career with Art Blakey and later moved on to play with Charles Lloyd and Miles Davis.

about_quotes.speaker: Mcbee
about_quotes.evidence: this is Jack DeJohnette, Keith Jarrett, myself and Charles Lloyd... playing with those guys

about_quotes.speaker: Jack DeJohnette
about_quotes.evidence: I had my old bands, other good musicians, Keith Jarrett and Gary Peacock’s Standards Trio.
about_quotes.doc_title: Jack DeJohnette interviewed by Monk Rowe, via Zoom, April 7, 2025

about_quotes.speaker: Marian McPartland
about_quotes.evidence: I met him at Tower Records one day... I met him in the store and needless to say, tried to nail him for a date.
about_quotes.doc_title: Marian McPartland — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: Mcbee
about_quotes.evidence: By now I’m playing literally around the world with Keith Jarrett, Jack DeJohnette, and Charles Lloyd.

about_quotes.speaker: Hubert Laws
about_quotes.evidence: [INTERVIEWER] ...when you’re young and a fan of, say, Chick Corea and Keith Jarrett, and they appear on the same album... I know, as a drummer, I would get anything Tony Williams put out... So when you look down and you see... Eumir Deodato, and then here we have Morning Star. When I saw the listing
about_quotes.doc_title: Hubert Laws — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: Håkan Rydin
about_quotes.evidence: I had four musicians at the generation of McCoy. It was Herbie Hancock, it was McCoy Tyner, it was Chick Corea and it was Keith Jarrett. ... Those were my four most important.
about_quotes.doc_title: Håkan Rydin interviewed by Monk Rowe, Glasgow, Scotland, July 27, 2016
